evals.prompt: I want to run eslint automatically before Claude commits code. How do I set up a hook for that?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step instructions for creating a PreToolUse hook that runs eslint before Bash git commit calls, including the correct JSON structure in settings.json and how the matcher/command fields work.
expectations: The response explains PreToolUse hooks (not just generic hooks)
The response includes a concrete settings.json example with the hook configuration
The response mentions the Bash tool matcher for intercepting git commit commands
The response explains how the hook command's exit code determines whether the tool call proceeds

evals.prompt: how do i add a postgres MCP server so claude can query my database directly?
evals.expected_output: Instructions for configuring an MCP server in .mcp.json or settings, with a concrete postgres example showing the server command, args, and how Claude discovers and uses the tools it exposes.
expectations: The response references .mcp.json as the configuration file for MCP servers
The response includes a concrete JSON config example for a postgres MCP server
The response explains the mcpServers object structure (command, args, env fields)
The response mentions that Claude auto-discovers tools exposed by the MCP server

evals.prompt: I need to run claude code in our CI pipeline to auto-review PRs. What are the CLI flags for non-interactive mode and how do I authenticate?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of headless/non-interactive mode with the -p flag, --output-format for structured output, authentication via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var, and practical CI usage patterns.
expectations: The response explains the -p (--print) flag for non-interactive/headless mode
The response mentions --output-format for structured output (JSON/stream-json)
The response covers authentication via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
The response provides a practical example of running claude in CI (e.g. a github actions snippet or shell command)

evals.prompt: i want to fork my current claude session so i can try two different approaches. how do i do that?
evals.expected_output: Instructions for forking/branching a session using /branch from within a session and claude --resume <session-id> --fork-session from the CLI.
expectations: The response mentions /branch as the in-session way to fork
The response mentions claude --resume <session-id> --fork-session as the CLI way
The response explains that forking creates a copy of the conversation that diverges from that point

evals.prompt: I have like 200 files that need the same migration applied. Is there a way to fan this out to multiple agents in parallel?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of /batch for fanning out parallelizable work across multiple worktree agents, plus git worktree support with claude -w.
expectations: The response mentions /batch as the command for fanning out large changesets
The response explains that /batch uses worktree agents to parallelize the work
The response mentions git worktrees or claude -w as the underlying mechanism for parallel isolation

evals.prompt: im working on a monorepo and need claude to see both the frontend and backend repos. how do i give it access to multiple folders?
evals.expected_output: Instructions for using --add-dir or /add-dir to give Claude access to additional directories beyond the current working directory.
expectations: The response mentions --add-dir as a CLI flag for adding directories
The response mentions /add-dir as an in-session command alternative
The response explains that adding a directory gives Claude both visibility and permissions for that path

evals.prompt: I want to create a custom agent that acts as a code reviewer with specific guidelines. How do I set up custom agents in claude code?
evals.expected_output: Instructions for defining custom agents in .claude/agents/ directory and running them with claude --agent=<name>, including the agent markdown format.
expectations: The response mentions .claude/agents/ as the directory for defining custom agents
The response mentions claude --agent=<name> as the way to invoke a custom agent
The response explains the agent file format (markdown with frontmatter or specific structure)

evals.prompt: can I use the desktop app to have claude start my dev server and test it in a browser? how does that work?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of the Claude Desktop app's ability to automatically start web servers and test them in a built-in browser, including how to set up similar functionality in CLI.
expectations: The response mentions the Desktop app's built-in browser or Chrome DevTools integration for testing web servers
The response explains how Claude can automatically start and interact with a web server
The response mentions how to set up similar functionality in the CLI (e.g. via MCP or chrome-devtools)
